title: Spot the atom-exhaustion vulnerability in a GenServer
prompt:
Below is a GenServer that registers worker processes:

```elixir
defmodule MyApp.WorkerSupervisor do
  def start_worker(user_id, worker_type) do
    name = String.to_atom("worker_#{user_id}_#{worker_type}")
    GenServer.start_link(MyApp.Worker, [user_id, worker_type], name: name)
  end
end
```

Identify the security vulnerability by name, explain why it is dangerous in the BEAM runtime, and produce a fixed version of the module. Write your analysis and the fixed code to `lib/my_app/worker_supervisor.ex`. The fix should use `Registry` with a string/binary key so that process names are never dynamically minted atoms. Include a short comment at the top of the file stating what was fixed.
tier_rationale: Short self-contained snippet with one well-known vulnerability. Requires identification plus a canonical Registry-based fix.
research_citation: Paraxial.io: 'Atoms in Elixir are not garbage collected... default limit 1_048_576.'
